project_overview.project_name: Access to Distributed Electricity and Lighting in Ethiopia (ADELE) Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase access to reliable electricity for households, social institutions, and enterprises in Ethiopia
project_overview.beneficiaries: 4,950,000 people provided with new or improved electricity service, 131,500 enterprises, 1,900 institutions

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - project will be implemented in conflict-affected and internal displacement areas, with over 920,000 refugees and 2.8 million internally displaced persons

key_components.component_name: Network strengthening for improved reliability of supply in urban areas
key_components.description: Rehabilitation and expansion of more than 600 km of MV lines, distribution network upgrades in Addis Ababa and 10 regional capitals, Revenue Protection Program implementation

key_components.component_name: Solar-hybrid mini grids for rural economic development
key_components.description: Rollout of solar mini-grids with battery storage and/or diesel backup through public and private sector approaches, targeting 240,000 connections

key_components.component_name: Solar home systems for households, small-holder farmers and small businesses
key_components.description: Expand availability and affordability of off-grid solar systems for 750,000 households through results-based financing and access to finance facilities

key_components.component_name: Standalone solar systems for health and education facilities
key_components.description: Supply and installation of stand-alone solar systems for 1,400 health centers and secondary schools in remote rural areas

key_components.component_name: Capacity building, technical assistance and implementation support
key_components.description: Technical assistance, capacity building, and implementation support for sector institutions, environmental and social management systems, gender equality programs

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Water, Irrigation and Energy (MoWIE), Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU), Development Bank of Ethiopia (DBE)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Steering committee led by MoWIE State Minister of Energy, comprising MoF, MoWIE, EEU, DBE and EEA for project oversight and coordination
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, mix of international and national competitive bidding, EPC contracts, performance-based grants, minimum subsidy tenders
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Secure web-based integrated data platform for remote monitoring, quarterly IFRs, annual progress reports, independent verification agents

identified_risks.risk_description: Political disruption and social unrest, military operation in Tigray, upcoming elections, policy and regulatory changes required for off-grid sector
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: World Bank monitoring of political situation, appropriate mitigation measures as needed

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 impacts on external and fiscal accounts, foreign currency shortages, pressure on Ethiopian Birr, inflation, deteriorating financial sector conditions
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project provides medium-term financing and increases financial sector capacity to serve off-grid sector capital needs

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience with off-grid technologies, sustainability of service delivery, capacity constraints at MoWIE and EEU, overstretched institutional capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Component 5 capacity building activities, technical assistance, specialized international and local experts, establishment of Off-Grid Unit at EEU

identified_risks.risk_description: High procurement risk, substantial FM risk, delays in external audit reports, internal control weaknesses, weak financial reporting
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FM action plan, procurement capacity building, training on World Bank regulations, strengthened audit mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Disposal and management of hazardous e-wastes from solar panels and batteries, limited capacity for waste management, occupational health and safety risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework, waste management plans, OHS plans, capacity building on environmental management

identified_risks.risk_description: Land acquisition and resettlement impacts, gender-based violence risks, labor influx, social exclusion of vulnerable groups, conflict tensions in IDP areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Resettlement Policy Framework, GBV action plan, stakeholder engagement plan, social assessment, grievance redress mechanism

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS5: Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM with multiple channels, dedicated GBV/SEA/SH procedures, accessible for all affected communities including historically underserved peoples
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Comprehensive gender strategy focusing on women's employment in energy sector (target 30% in mini-grids, 28% in off-grid companies), productive uses of energy for women, access to finance for women entrepreneurs, capacity building
vulnerable_groups: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African historically underserved traditional local communities

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project will deploy conflict lens and conflict tool to understand situation and design appropriate mitigation measures, alert World Bank of security incidents and potential conflict escalation
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia hosts 920,000+ refugees from Somalia, Sudan, South Sudan, Eritrea; 2.8 million IDPs; political instability and social unrest; military operation in Tigray region; accessibility challenges for monitoring in conflict areas
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Political disruption and social unrest could affect project implementation, potential civil unrest limiting accessibility of subprojects for monitoring, security incidents to be reported to World Bank
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - project operates in areas with IDPs and refugee-hosting communities, need to be cognizant of dynamics to ensure no escalation of tensions, project design considers IDP settlements and refugee areas
